Empires and Puzzles Emilio Review: 5-Star Legendary Fire Red Hero (Starfall Circus)
Emilio was once the speed king of ailment immunity. Does his base form still hold up, or has he been left in the dust by modern stats? We break down his unique mechanics.
Emilio Review: The Original Ailment Blocker

Emilio is a Legendary Fire hero from the Starfall Circus event, originally released in June 2021. In his prime, he was a game-changer, introducing Very Fast ailment immunity that could shut down entire enemy teams before they could even sneeze. He’s a Druid who thrives on momentum—once he gets going, his team becomes nearly untouchable. This review focuses on his
, analyzing whether his unique mechanics can still shine despite his aging stats compared to his flashy Costume and modern powerhouses.

Emilio sports a classic "Ringmaster of the Undead" look. With his top hat, skeletal face paint, and confident pose, he looks like he's about to introduce the main act—which turns out to be setting you on fire. It’s a theatrical, slightly creepy design that fits the Starfall Circus theme perfectly. Compared to his Costume, which goes full "Swamp Thing" with a frog mask, the base version retains a certain elegant, albeit skeletal, charm. He definitely has better fashion sense than the frog version.

Let's be blunt: Emilio's base stats are from 2021, and it shows. With a base Power of 809 and Attack/Defense hovering around 750-800, he is significantly behind modern heroes who regularly push 1260+ Power and 1300+ stats. Without emblems and Limit Breaks (and ideally the Costume Bonus), he is incredibly fragile by today's standards. He relies entirely on his Very Fast speed and defensive utility to survive, because he certainly isn't tanking hits with his raw health pool anymore.
Emilio’s special skill is a "conditional" mechanic that rewards timing and synergy.
If enemies are already burning, he unleashes his full potential: a stronger burn, a heal-over-time for allies, and the coveted status ailment immunity.
Without existing burns, he applies a weaker burn and the immunity.
3-turn status ailment immunity at Very Fast speed
is the crown jewel. It effectively blocks Alfrike, Bubbles, and other ailment-heavy heroes if you can fire just 6 tiles into the match.
The healing (549 over 3 turns) is nice but not enough to sustain a team under heavy fire on its own, especially with modern damage outputs.
Very Fast (6.5 tiles). With a Level 11 Mana Troop (or even lower with costume bonus/emblems), he charges in
These numbers are incredibly low by modern standards where heroes deal 1000+ damage instantly. He is not a killer; he is a disruptor.
183 HP per turn (Conditional). Decent for topping up, but requires the conditional trigger.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Starfall Circus family bonus provides a self-cleanse mechanism, which is excellent for a support hero who needs to keep casting.

The Starfall family bonus (50%/75%/100% chance to cleanse the most recent ailment) makes him slippery. Even if he gets hit with a silence or mana block, there's a coin flip (or better) chance he shakes it off at the end of the turn. This is crucial for maintaining his loop of immunity.
The base version does not have the "Copy Ailment" passive found on his Costume. He relies solely on his Family Bonus and Class.

is a solid, albeit boring, choice for him. Since his base stats are low, any extra defense at the start of battle helps him survive long enough to fire his first skill. It’s functional but doesn't have the playmaking potential of something like

Upon release, Emilio was a top-tier war hero. His ability to block ailments at Very Fast speed made him a premier counter to the slow, ailment-heavy "Rush Attack" meta and heroes like Gravemaker or Telluria.
His stats have fallen off a cliff. While his skill
(Very Fast Immunity) remains S-tier utility, his

you can keep him alive. However, he has been largely superseded by his own Costume (which has better stats) and newer heroes like Hathor or Toon Vivica who offer immunity with much bulkier frames.
This is the elephant in the room.
The Costume gives a massive stat boost (approx +400 Power). Base Emilio is barely usable without the Costume Bonus stats.

(higher HoT). If you own the Costume but need more healing, running the Base version with the Costume Bonus stats is a legitimate strategy.
Emilio’s job is to survive and cast. He does negligible direct damage, so Attack power is wasted on him (it only slightly increases his DoT).
Maximizes his low HP pool and bolstering his defense ensures he survives slash attacks.

You can't rely on him as a solo healer if you can't trigger the burn condition.
His damage output is negligible in the current meta.
His speed is capped, but his immunity is invaluable against Alfrikes.

Specifically for countering slow, ailment-heavy defenses.
Emilio is an "engine" hero. He needs fuel (other burners) to run at max efficiency.

If you pull him, congrats! He is a high-skill, high-reward hero. Focus on keeping him alive. Don't worry about his damage; use him to protect your team from nasty status effects.
You likely have him on the bench unless you have the Costume. If you

have the Costume, consider running the Base version in situations where you need slightly more healing (549 vs 450 HP). The Druid class minions can also be more annoying than the Wizard talent on defense.
On release, players called him "Overpowered" solely for the Very Fast immunity, comparing him to a "Red Garnet."
"Total burn damage looks high... but it is distributed along much more turns." The community quickly realized he wasn't a nuker but a sustainer.
Many players initially struggled to choose between him and Gravemaker. History has shown Emilio's immunity often ages better than raw damage, though modern stats challenge that notion.
Base Emilio is a classic "utility over power" hero. While his stats are gathering dust in a museum, his

remains a premier skill. He’s like an old reliable umbrella—it might look tattered, but it still keeps the rain (and poison, and fire, and mindless attack) off your head. If you have the Costume to boost his stats, the Base version remains a viable, tactical choice for those who value healing over raw damage.
A fragile tactical nuke of utility.
A comprehensive review of Emilio, the Very Fast Fire hero from Starfall Circus. We analyze his ailment immunity, healing, and viability in the modern era vs his Costume.
